title: bitter glassy-jelly german cabbage pasta
description: a unique and complex date-night dish, featuring the bitter flavors of napa cabbage and a glassy, jelly-like pasta-water-emulsion.

ingredients.name: pasta water
ingredients.notes: cooled slightly, with 2 tbsp of butter dissolved into it

ingredients.name: mustard seeds
ingredients.notes: brown or white, toasted in a dry pan for 2 minutes

instructions: bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta until al dente, then drain and set aside.

in a blender or food processor, combine the cooled pasta water, chicken or vegetable stock, white wine vinegar, honey, mustard seeds, and coriander seeds, blending until a smooth, glassy emulsion forms.
transfer the emulsion to a saucepan and warm it gently over low heat.
in another pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat and add the shredded napa cabbage, cooking until tender and slightly caramelized.
remove the cabbage from the heat and season with fleur de sel to taste.
to assemble the dish, place a portion of cooked pasta on a plate, followed by a spoonful of the glassy emulsion, and top with the cooked cabbage.
if desired, brush the edges of the pasta with an egg wash made from the beaten eggs and a tablespoon of flour, then sprinkle with additional fleur de sel to taste.
(optional) for an added layer of flavor, sprinkle a pinch of toasted mustard seeds and coriander seeds on top of the dish before serving.
